jobs.basic_qualifications:

jobs.description:
Amazon is looking for a German Language Analyst with at least 2+ years BPO experience in a large customer-oriented corporate environment for our B2B program. Amazon Business is one of Amazon’s most exciting growth opportunities. Since its launch in 2015, Amazon Business (AB) already has more than 2 million customers. We are growing fast, and require talented professionals to help us scale.
This role will require for the successful candidate to:
• Collect and analyze quantitative data
• Answer queries, investigate and resolve issues and questions from customers through inbound calls and emails
• Work efficiently with customers and provide world-class service
• Analyze trends and suggest process improvements
• Meet the set KRAs
• Perform other projects and duties as required
• Communicate results to business leaders with Finance, Accounting, Operations and Business Development teams.

Working Conditions:
• Open office space on site
• Report daily to the Pasay office
• The team generally follows European time. However, you may be requested to work on shifting schedules including night shift, if business requires
• Work during PH Holidays is required when business supported is operating
• Overtime may be required on critical and peak periods
Internal job description
Amazon is looking for a German Language Analyst with at least 2+ years BPO experience in a large customer-oriented corporate environment, for our B2B business which is one of Amazon’s most exciting growth opportunities. Since its launch in 2015, Amazon Business (AB) already has more than 2 million customers. We are growing fast, and require talented professionals to help us scale.
This role will require for the successful candidate to:
• Collect and analyze quantitative data
• 80% of the time will be involved in answering queries received from customers through inbound calls and emails
• Investigate to resolve issues sent by the customers
• Work efficiently with customers and provide world-class service
• Analyze trends and suggest process improvements
• Meet the set KRAs
• Perform other projects and duties as required
• Communicate results to business leaders with Finance, Accounting, Operations and Business Development teams.

Working Conditions:
• Open office space on site
• Report daily to the Pasay office
• The team generally follows European time. However, you may be requested to work on shifting schedules including night shift, if business requires
• Work during PH Holidays is required when business supported is operating
• Overtime may be required on critical and peak periods


Key job responsibilities
Knowledgeable in Accounts Receivable Cash application process.
- Collecting and analyzing quantitative data
- Use data analyses and statistical techniques to develop solutions to guide business decision making
- Identify predictors and causes of business related problems and implement appropriate corrective solutions
- Identify, develop, manage and execute analyses to uncover areas of opportunity and present written business recommendations
- Collaborate with multiple teams as a leader of quantitative analysis and develop solutions that utilize the highest standards of analytical rigor and data integrity
- Analyze and solve business problems at their root
- Analyze trends and savings initiatives
- Communicating results to business leaders with Finance, Accounting, Operations and Business Development
- Perform other projects and duties as required
- Supports team manager and performs management duties when manager is absent or out of office

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience in project management in data centers or comparable critical infrastructure
- Experience reading, interpreting, and creating construction drawings, specifications, and submittal documents
- Experience with AutoCAD, Revit, Bluebeam, and MS Office Suite
- * 5+ years Information and Communications Technology (ICT) design experience

jobs.description:
As part of the AWS Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

As an Information and Communications Technology (ICT) Design Engineer you will work with multiple design disciplines, network engineering teams, technical deployment engineering and management, and operations teams to bridge gaps, and support design and deployment of ICT infrastructure in Amazon Physical Stores. Amazon Stores are the first grocery stores to offer Just Walk Out (JWO) Technology. These are stores with advanced shopping technology so you never have to wait in line. Our checkout-free shopping experience is made possible by our JWO Technology, which automatically detects when products are taken from, or returned to the shelves, and keeps track of them in a virtual cart. When you’re done shopping, you can just leave the store. Shortly after, we’ll charge your Amazon account and send you a receipt.

The position requires strong working knowledge in a wide variety of building types and systems (both the design of such systems and also installation and maintenance). Candidates must be capable of making creative design recommendations and must have the ability to articulate design justification to different groups within the organization. This position will be part of an exciting, fast-moving ambiguous business unit, where everyone on the team needs to be entrepreneurial, wear many hats and work effectively in a highly collaborative environment.

As a member of this team, you will work directly with engineers and managers across the globe to develop connectivity solutions for the physical layer. You will work with multiple teams to design and plan ICT network infrastructure to include space, pathway, power, cooling, wireless and cabling design specifications for construction and permitting. You will work with industry suppliers and manufacturers to develop product requirements and recommend new technologies. You will work with and manage third party ICT design consultants and Low Voltage Contractors (LVC).

You are the right person for this job if you have a strong end user focus and a passion for producing high-quality work. You must be a self-starter, creative, and a critical thinker with an intense curiosity and drive for learning. You must demonstrate the ability to efficiently plan and prioritize all deliverables and resources across multiple projects and working groups spanning design and technology. This position requires you to manage stakeholder expectations, schedules, budgets, assets and scopes—because organization and attention to detail are in your DNA. You will work closely with internal teams and external partners. You will make decisions and find solutions quickly and with confidence, and not expect someone else to show you the right way forward. You are the right person for this position if you take ownership of your work, display a great deal of initiative, possess common sense, sound judgment, and a sense of humor.

As an ICT Design Engineer you will:
- Collaborate with MEP Designers, Network Engineers, Architects, Design managers, Procurement teams and Implementation Managers to coordinate design and deployment of IoT devices and ICT infrastructure to include power, cooling, space, pathways, cabinets, racks, and network equipment
- Review, approve and stamp ICT product data submittals with RCDD, P.E. or other ICT industry certified Stamp
- Develop, establish and revise ICT BODs, SOPs, and Construction Specifications
- Work with BIM teams to create, edit, and revise design drawing details and templates
- Attend design meetings, and review, comment and approve ICT design drawings and specifications using Bluebeam or other PDF software
- Analyze, optimize, troubleshoot and improve Wi-Fi network designs
- Write 1 pager and 6 pager technical documents with analytical cost data and present to executive leadership to propose changes in ICT designs
- Work with procurement teams, product manufacturers and distributors to develop maintain and manage Global ICT Product equipment lists, pricing and lead times
- Develop and provide RFP, SOW and Bid Templates to ICT design consultants and LVC - Review, analyze, level and compare bids
- Manage ICT design consultants and LVC Contractors holding them accountable for on time, high quality deliverables
- Identify and respond to risks and high-priority issues rapidly and effectively
- Support new technology development and design activities that improve infrastructure quality, reduce cost, and installation time
- Travel 25+% to review projects, coordinate with onsite personnel and contractors at JWO 3P Stores
- Have fun and offer creative out of the box solutions

This is an opportunity to leverage and grow your existing experience in an innovative and highly collaborative environment where the results of your work will directly impact Amazon and its customers.


Key job responsibilities
As an ICT Design Engineer you will:
- Collaborate with MEP Designers, Network Engineers, Architects, Design managers, Procurement teams and Implementation Managers to coordinate design and deployment of IoT devices and ICT infrastructure to include power, cooling, space, pathways, cabinets, racks, and network equipment
- Review, approve and stamp ICT product data submittals with RCDD, P.E. or other ICT industry certified Stamp
- Develop, establish and revise ICT BODs, SOPs, and Construction Specifications
- Work with BIM teams to create, edit, and revise design drawing details and templates
- Attend design meetings, and review, comment and approve ICT design drawings and specifications using Bluebeam or other PDF software
- Analyze, optimize, troubleshoot and improve Wi-Fi network designs
- Write 1 pager and 6 pager technical documents with analytical cost data and present to executive leadership to propose changes in ICT designs
- Work with procurement teams, product manufacturers and distributors to develop maintain and manage Global ICT Product equipment lists, pricing and lead times
- Develop and provide RFP, SOW and Bid Templates to ICT design consultants and LVC
- Review, analyze, level and compare bids
- Manage ICT design consultants and LVC Contractors holding them accountable for on time, high quality deliverables
- Identify and respond to risks and high-priority issues rapidly and effectively
- Support new technology development and design activities that improve infrastructure quality, reduce cost, and installation time
- Travel 25+% to review projects, coordinate with onsite personnel and contractors at JWO 3P Stores
- Have fun and offer creative out of the box solutions


A day in the life
Inclusive Team Culture
Here at Amazon, we embrace our differences. We are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We have ten employee-led affinity groups, reaching 40,000 employees in over 190 chapters globally. We have innovative benefit offerings, and host annual and ongoing learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.

Work/Life Balance
Our team puts a high value on work-life balance. It isn’t about how many hours you spend at home or at work; it’s about the flow you establish that brings energy to both parts of your life. We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign opportunities based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded contributor.

About the team
As part of the AWS Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

jobs.description:
Are you a full-cycle learning professional with proven experience delivering innovative learning solutions? Are you excited by invention and innovation? Do you thrive in bringing technology solutions to third party customers to grow and evolve their businesses?

Our Just Walk Out technology Global Education team is seeking an innovative, analytical, and full cycle Sr. Learning Program Manager to define, build and implement game-changing, just-in-time learning solutions at scale. Our mission is to be Earth's most innovative and accessible knowledge provider - helping Just Walk Out technology internal and external partners unlock the future of retail. We deliver world-class, scalable knowledge solutions that simplify even the most complex concepts. Our solutions provide users actionable insights at their moment of need, and boost performance to reach KPI targets.


Key job responsibilities
In partnership with your Global Education peers, you will deliver strategic, innovative, scalable knowledge solutions to support our Just Walk Out technology customers in achieving their business priorities. You will define business and end-user learning requirements, and partner with technology experts – internal and external – to deploy embedded learning capabilities to our diverse audiences.

You will collaborate with subject matter experts to design and maintain knowledge resources which enable moment of need self-service knowledge acquisition and which drive measurable performance impact. You will ensure solutions support friction-free access through all needed media (Guided workflows, AR, VR, and other digital learning) and tools (3P tools, phones, tablets, computers). You will apply program management rigor to establish and deliver solutions on time, and use high judgement to ruthlessly prioritize bandwidth.

A day in the life
You dive deep into details with subject matter experts to define end user personas and performance requirements. You develop globally scalable, repeatable knowledge and skill acquisition strategies that are easily adopted, and optimized for defect-free performance execution.

You leverage your deep and broad experience across knowledge solution modalities to meet end users where they are, integrate seamlessly with tech solutions and operational processes, and measurably enable customers to achieve targeted business impact outcomes.

You relentlessly seek out voice of customer feedback to validate real-world efficacy and continuously improve solutions. You use a combination of objective data and anecdote to continuously improve end-user experience and deliver on our customer promise.

You thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and operate with a high sense of urgency to deliver results on time and on target.

About the team
As part of the Applied AI Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

jobs.description:
Are you an experienced full-cycle learning program and people manager? Do you have proven experience prioritizing team capacity to measurably deliver on key business metrics? Do you thrive in bringing bar raising knowledge solutions to end users at their moment of need? Are you excited by innovative technology solutions that grow and evolve for third party customer businesses?

Our Just Walk Out technology Global Education team is seeking an innovative, analytical, Manager of Learning Programs who can build and implement game-changing, just-in-time knowledge solutions at scale, while also leading a team of local Learning Program Managers.

Our mission is to be Earth's most innovative and accessible knowledge provider - helping Just Walk Out technology internal and external partners unlock the future of retail. We deliver world-class, scalable knowledge solutions that simplify even the most complex concepts. Our solutions provide users actionable insights at their moment of need, and boost performance to reach KPI targets.

Key job responsibilities
In partnership with your Global Education peers, you will deliver strategic, innovative, scalable knowledge solutions to support our Just Walk Out technology customers in achieving their business priorities. You will define business and end-user learning requirements for defined workstreams, and partner with product stakeholders and experts to strategize and prioritize deliverables. You will use high judgement and strong stakeholder relationships to ruthlessly prioritize bandwidth and build capacity roadmaps for assigned workstreams.

You will ensure solutions support friction-free access through all needed media (guided workflows, AR, VR, and other digital learning) and tools (3P tools, phones, tablets, computers).

You will manage the performance of an assigned group of Learning Program Managers to execute to standard work and deliver solutions on time, Insist on the Highest Standards for each deliverable, and achieve measurable impact. You and your team will collaborate with subject matter experts to design and deploy embedded knowledge solutions across our diverse audiences which enable moment of need self-service knowledge acquisition, and surface impact metrics.

A day in the life
You thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment, operate with a high rigor and sense of urgency to drive your team and yourself to Deliver Results on time and on target.

You dive deep into details with stakeholders and product owners to anticipate user persona needs and performance requirements. You use collaborative influence with these stakeholders to ruthlessly prioritize deliverable roadmaps within constrained bandwidth.

You partner with Global Education team Learning Program Managers to develop strategic, globally scalable, repeatable knowledge and skill acquisition strategies that are easily adopted, and optimized for defect-free performance execution.

You leverage your deep and broad experience across knowledge solution modalities to meet end users where they are, integrate seamlessly with tech solutions and operational processes, and measurably enable customers to achieve targeted business impact outcomes.

You relentlessly seek out voice of customer feedback – internal and external – to validate real-world efficacy and continuously improve solutions. You use a combination of objective data and anecdote to continuously improve end-user experience and deliver on our customer promise.

About the team
As part of the Applied AI Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

jobs.description:
As part of the AWS Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

Amazon Physical Stores is more start-up than big-company, a group of entrepreneurial, analytical, and creative leaders with innovation at our core. We’re pushing the state of the art in helping customers shop in engaging, fast, and safe ways. To date we’ve created stores that let you use an app to enter, take what you want from our fresh selection, and go (Amazon Go); a smart shopping cart that uses computer vision algorithms and sensor fusion to let you skip the checkout line (Amazon Dash Cart); and contactless services that let you pay, enter or identify yourself (Amazon One). Note: The specific team is internally and tech focused to delivery scalable solutions.

Our checkout-free shopping experience is made possible by our Just Walk Out Technology, which automatically detects when products are taken from or returned to the shelves and keeps track of them in a cart. When you’re done shopping, you can just leave the store. Shortly after, we’ll charge your Amazon account and send you a receipt. Check it out at amazon.com/go. Designed and custom-built by Amazonians, our Just Walk Out Technology uses a variety of technologies including computer vision, sensor fusion, and advanced machine learning. Innovation is part of our DNA! Our goal is to be Earths’ most customer centric company and we are just getting started. We need people who want to join an ambitious program that continues to push the state of the art in computer vision, machine learning, distributed systems and hardware design.

We are looking for a Business Analyst with a passion for using data to discover and solve real world problems. You will enjoy working with one of the richest data sets in the world, latest technology, and the ability to see your insights drive the creation of JWO stores.

The perfect candidate will have passion and experience analyzing data and using that analysis to drive key insights and recommendations. As a business analyst you will also build reports and metrics, drive ad hoc analysis and communicate insights to key stakeholders. You will not only execute on the required skills but also be able to influence, educate, and drive results in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment.

Key job responsibilities
Continually improve ongoing reporting and analysis processes, automating or simplifying self-service support for customers
Interface with business customers, gathering requirements and delivering complete reporting solutions
Interface with other technology teams to extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from a wide variety of data sources

A day in the life
This position will closely work with CXQO operations team to develop dashboards to analyze data for determining root cause, building business insights and dive deep.
It involves complex analysis and diving deep into key metrics based on requests, building and maintaining multiple databases.



About the team
We have BIEs and DEs in our team to support analytics,data and reporting needs for DS, SDE and PMs across JWO team

jobs.description:
As part of the AWS Applied AI Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

Join us on our mission to revolutionize the way the world shops. We are the Amazon Physical Stores Technical Operations team, responsible for launching and operating the commerce software services which run stores with its “Just Walk Out” technology.

Our approach to problems is entrepreneurial. You will be challenged to invent, create and solve challenging problems as well as wear many hats and collaborate in an environment that’s more startup than big company. You will need a strong ability to troubleshoot hardware, software, and network issues.

As a Support Engineer in JWO Tech Operations team, you seek resolution to problems and mitigate risk, always ensuring a Customer Obsessed experience has occurred. You will be working on services with a direct impact on the customer experience. If you are excited about the opportunity to learn and work on distributed systems, enjoy trouble shooting and solving complex problems, consider the opportunities to work with Amazon Physical Stores.

You will help solve a variety of challenges and offer your expertise in growing the knowledge of your peers via team collaboration. You will be counted on to identify areas of improvement and drive projects to implement them. We consistently whiteboard so be comfortable writing and supporting your ideas on the team board. You will play an active role in defining the support processes for technologies in partnership with other technology leaders within and possibly outside the team. You should be comfortable with a level of ambiguity that’s higher than most projects and relish the idea of solving big challenges. You will also mentor other engineers in your area of expertise. Along the way, we guarantee that you’ll work hard, have fun and impact many customers!

This role requires the flexibility to work 5 days a week (occasionally on weekends) on a rotational basis. AWS Support is 24x7x365 operations and work timings for this role is in India night time i.e. 10 PM to 6 AM IST or 1 PM to 10 PM IST. You are expected to work in night shifts hours based on business requirements.



About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

- Experience troubleshooting and debugging technical systems
jobs.description:
As part of the AWS Applied AI Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

Join us on our mission to revolutionize the way the world shops. We are the Amazon Physical Stores Technical Operations team, responsible for launching and operating the commerce software services which run stores with its “Just Walk Out” technology.

Our approach to problems is entrepreneurial. You will be challenged to invent, create and solve challenging problems as well as wear many hats and collaborate in an environment that’s more startup than big company. You will need a strong ability to troubleshoot hardware, software, and network issues.

As a Support Engineer in JWO Tech Operations team, you seek resolution to problems and mitigate risk, always ensuring a Customer Obsessed experience has occurred. You will be working on services with a direct impact on the customer experience. If you are excited about the opportunity to learn and work on distributed systems, enjoy trouble shooting and solving complex problems, consider the opportunities to work with Amazon Physical Stores.

You will help solve a variety of challenges and offer your expertise in growing the knowledge of your peers via team collaboration. You will be counted on to identify areas of improvement and drive projects to implement them. We consistently whiteboard so be comfortable writing and supporting your ideas on the team board. You will play an active role in defining the support processes for technologies in partnership with other technology leaders within and possibly outside the team. You should be comfortable with a level of ambiguity that’s higher than most projects and relish the idea of solving big challenges. You will also mentor other engineers in your area of expertise. Along the way, we guarantee that you’ll work hard, have fun and impact many customers!

This role requires the flexibility to work 5 days a week (occasionally on weekends) on a rotational basis. AWS Support is 24x7x365 operations and work timings for this role is in India night time i.e. 10 PM to 6 AM IST or 1 PM to 10 PM IST. You are expected to work in night shifts hours based on business requirements.



About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.
Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.title: Support Engineer III, Just Walk Out Tech

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Experience in Design/Construction using Autodesk Revit to design and coordinate large, complex, or innovative projects
- Experience coding, scripting, automating in at least one language (e.g., Python, C, C++, Java, Ruby, or PowerShell)
- Experience with reporting and Data Visualization tools such as Quick Sight / Tableau / Power BI or other BI packages
- Experience using one or more of the following technologies (e.g., AutoCAD, Rhino, Revit, SolidWorks, CREO, BIM360, SketchUp, KeyShot, etc.) to deliver high quality models and designs
- Experience with hands-on integrations including custom map development, API development, XSD, REST, SOAP based services
- Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team, or experience leading the architecture and design (architecture, design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and current systems
- Experience in deploying identity and access management systems, or experience that includes strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication abilities and experience managing full application stacks from the OS up through custom applications
- Data-Driven Process Improvement: Expertise in gathering and analyzing enterprise-level BIM datasets to identify inefficiencies and deliver continuous business improvements across large-scale portfolios.
- Senior Coordination & Delivery: Proven experience leading the design and coordination of large, complex, or innovative projects, with a focus on BIM-to-Field execution and clash resolution.
- Specialized AEC Toolset: Advanced proficiency in industry-standard software:
- UNIFI/Content Catalog: For enterprise-level BIM content management and standard enforcement.
- Smartsheet: For asset tracking and schedule integration.
- API Integration & Development: Demonstrated ability to implement and manage API-based integrations (specifically Revit API, Smartsheet API, and Autodesk Platform Services/Forge) to bridge BIM data with external enterprise datasets.
jobs.description:
As part of the AWS Applied AI Solutions organization, we have a vision to provide business applications, leveraging Amazon’s unique experience and expertise, that are used by millions of companies worldwide to manage day-to-day operations. We will accomplish this by accelerating our customers’ businesses through delivery of intuitive and differentiated technology solutions that solve enduring business challenges. We blend vision with curiosity and Amazon’s real-world experience to build opinionated, turnkey solutions. Where customers prefer to buy over build, we become their trusted partner with solutions that are no-brainers to buy and easy to use.

Amazon Just Walk Out as a Service (JWOS) delivers solutions to our Retail Customers to simplify and improve the shopping experience. Our enterprise-ready autonomous retail technology enables shoppers to simply enter a store, grab what they want, and just go. Just Walk Out uses a combination of technologies to eliminate checkout lines and bring fast and convenient checkout experiences to shoppers.

Are you excited about working with ground-breaking technology in Retail? Are you customer-obsessed and detail oriented? Do you enjoy working with external customers? Do you have experience working with both design and technology development teams to meet daily business requirements?

Amazon is seeking a highly motivated and organized Sr. BIM Manager who drives the overall BIM strategy for the JWOS Launch. BIM strategy at JWOS requires a balance between internal stakeholder requirements, BIM content user requirements, and JWOS BIM and Standards for both the internal Design teams and external third party (3P) partners. The Sr. BIM Manager drives the BIM and workflow strategy to address design documentation challenges, increase overall efficiency between JWO Launch sub-teams, and increase span of control for Design teams. This role is responsible for building the flow of information from 3P Customers through the Launch Teams (ICT, Camera Planning, and Design Management) in the development, maintenance, and distribution of the JWOS Design Catalog and execution. An ideal candidate is a detail-oriented self-starter who can also step back and see the big picture. This person thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and has a deep passion for developing and implementing standards, tools, and processes across a growing organization. Strong project management and communication skills, both written and verbal are required. People management experience preferred.


Key job responsibilities
- Influence and lead data strategy frameworks and ability to strategize data interoperability solutions involving enterprise platform and software (e.g. Autodesk) and proprietary systems.
- Translate new product technical requirements for internal and external stakeholders BIM technology use
- Support BIM design coordination workflows using current industry standard technologies
- Support and consult on team needs for Revit project setup, template maintenance, project troubleshooting
- Develop custom automation tools and plugins using C# or Python to extend the functionality of BIM software, specifically leveraging the Revit API and other Autodesk APIs to streamline design workflows.
- Driving and maintaining standards and process and tool operating procedures
- Reality Capture workflows as it relates to building design and operations phases
- Experience with 3D Scanning or Photogrammetry
- Interest and ability to deep dive in metadata for organizing and pipeline creation based on cross functional team needs
- Stays up-to-date with new BIM technologies and ability to evaluate, roadmap, deploy in cross-functional team workstreams and adapt to business requirements as needed
- Reporting program roadmaps and timelines to peers and senior management
- Minimum 10 years experience in BIM management within a major design or AEC firm, with a proven track record of establishing enterprise-wide digital standards, managing multidisciplinary teams, and leading the transition from traditional workflows to highly automated, data-driven BIM environments.
- Proven ability to manage complex asset metadata across fragmented platforms, with specific experience using APIs or custom scripting to connect BIM model parameters to external databases, schedules, or Facility Management (FM) systems.
- Establish and maintain a unified digital asset strategy across a nationwide retail fleet, ensuring the seamless integration of BIM geometry with external asset management databases to support real-time facility operations and capital planning for hundreds of store locations.

A day in the life
Inclusive Team Culture
Here at Amazon, we embrace our differences. We are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We have ten employee-led affinity groups, reaching 40,000 employees in over 190 chapters globally. We have innovative benefit offerings, and host annual and ongoing learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.

Work/Life Balance
Our team puts a high value on work-life balance. It isn’t about how many hours you spend at home or at work; it’s about the flow you establish that brings energy to both parts of your life. We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign opportunities based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded contributor.

About the team
Amazon Just Walk Out as a Service (JWOS) delivers solutions to our Retail Customers to simplify and improve the shopping experience. Our enterprise-ready autonomous retail technology enables shoppers to simply enter a store, grab what they want, and just go. Just Walk Out uses a combination of technologies to eliminate checkout lines and bring fast and convenient checkout experiences to shoppers.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of increasing responsibilities in EHS or environmental programs in manufacturing, production, or service operations experience
- Bachelor's degree
- Experience leading multiple direct reports or multiple sites
jobs.description:
One focus area of the Amazon Workplace Health & Safety (WHS) mission is to provide tools, resources, and environments that support safe, efficient, healthy behaviors and encourage employees to proactively manage their health and wellbeing. To support this focus area, Amazon is seeking an experienced and innovative Workplace Health and Safety (WHS) Manager to join our team.

The Site WHS Manager will be responsible for partnering with site operations teams to execute company WHS policies and ensure compliance to all applicable local and regional regulations. By leveraging lean principles and Kaizan’s, you will lead continuous improvement initiatives to reduce conditional and ergonomic risk in our processes to ensure a safe and healthful working environment for our Associates. A successful WHS manager will demonstrate the ability to build trust and confidence with the Operations Teams and direct reports and inspire change through providing comprehensive risk assessments and WHS data analysis.

The WHS Manager must be an effective communicator and send clear, concise and consistent messages, both verbally and in writing. You will instruct and train Operations Leaders and WHS team members in Amazon WHS policies and assist the operations site teams in incorporating our WHS standards at their site. You will work to identify best practices and incorporate these standard methodologies into our standards to continuously improve the WHS landscape. They will possess excellent WHS program knowledge as well as Workplace and ergonomic knowledge and be able demonstrate this expertise when working with Operations.

WHS Manager should deal with WHS site POC's and other operations stakeholders that need to understand the site situation and provide hands on training in the field. You will create and execute leadership development plans for your WHS Associates on the team. They will communicate WHS team expectations and give frequent and appropriate feedback to their WHS teams and ensure they are executing the core competencies of an Amazon WHS professional.

Basic Qualifications
· 5+ years of growing responsibilities in EHS or WHS and/or Workplace programs in manufacturing, production, or service operations.
· Experience implementing lean principles and process improvement in an operational environment.
· Experience leading multiple direct reports and/or multiple sites
· Bachelor’s degree in safety, environmental, ergonomics or a related field required
· Experience with OSHA and other health and WHS regulatory requirements

Preferred qualifications
· Distribution Center or Manufacturing WHS experience with mix of exempt and non-exempt employees at a site of at least 250 people preferred.
· Master’s degree preferred
· Experience in a fast paced, changing/growing organization
· Certified EHS/HSE/WHS Professional preferred
· Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including comfort collaborating with site leaders
· Ability to develop and implement department goals and strategies
· Strong analytical skills with demonstrated problem solving ability
· Ability to adapt well to fast-paced environments with changing circumstances, direction, and strategy
